tasks.name: Repo & CI readiness (pytest + node test/eslint harness, ci-gate)
tasks.description:
**task-00 â€” Repo & CI Readiness (the testing contract)**

**Objective**

Stand up the test/lint harnesses and CI gate that every subsequent task must
satisfy. Nothing else. This task defines HOW future tasks prove correctness:
`pytest` for the Python bot (with Supabase + Slack fully mocked, no network) and
`node --test` + `eslint` for the Node dashboard, both run by a GitHub Actions
`ci-gate` workflow.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/` â€” add a Python test harness:
  - `sources/bot/requirements-dev.txt` (pytest; anything else needed for tests).
  - `sources/bot/tests/conftest.py` â€” pytest fixtures that MOCK the Supabase
    client and the Slack HTTP layer so `handlers.py` / `supabase_service.py` /
    `slack_client.py` are importable and unit-testable WITHOUT any network or
    real `secrets.json`. Provide a fake/stub `secrets.json` (or monkeypatch
    `config`) so `import config` does not fail. Never use real credentials.
  - `sources/bot/tests/test_smoke.py` â€” a trivial test proving the harness and
    the mocks import cleanly (e.g. `handle_help` can be called with a mocked
    event and the mocked `send_ â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- `cd sources/bot && pip install -r requirements-dev.txt && pytest` passes with
  ZERO network access (mocks only).
- `cd sources/dashboard && npm install && npm run lint && npm test` passes.
- The `ci-gate` workflow has a Python job and a Node job; both must pass for the
  gate to be green.
- No real secrets committed; `secrets.json` used by tests is a stub/fixture.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md (context), README.md (hybrid model), `docs/AFDT-HANDOFF-PROCESS.md`.
- `sources/bot/config.py` (how secrets load â€” you must stub this for tests).
- `sources/dashboard/services/supabase.js` (the client you must mock).

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-00.md)

tasks.name: Data model: validate migration vs scratch DB + data-layer shims
tasks.description:
**task-01 â€” Data Model (validate migration + data-layer shims)**

**Objective**

Prove the Phase-0 migration is valid and give both surfaces a data-layer shim
for the new columns/table so later tasks have a stable API to build on. NO prod
writes â€” validation runs against a local scratch database in CI only.

**Files in scope**

- `db/001_phase0_migration.sql` â€” do NOT rewrite the schema; you may only fix
  outright SQL errors discovered during scratch-DB validation. The intended
  schema is authoritative in SPEC.md.
- CI validation: extend `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (or add a dedicated job)
  that spins up a **local scratch Postgres** (e.g. the `postgres` service
  container) and runs `db/001_phase0_migration.sql` against an EMPTY scratch
  schema to prove it applies cleanly and is idempotent (run it twice). Create
  minimal stub tables (`requests`, `mercury_users`) in the scratch DB first so
  the `ALTER TABLE` statements have something to alter. This job must NEVER
  point at production Supabase â€” it uses only th â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- CI's scratch-DB job applies the migration twice with no error (idempotent).
- `add_history(...)` calls the (mocked) Supabase client with a well-formed
  `request_history` row; unit test proves it.
- Dashboard shim returns `[]` for missing attachments and parses a populated
  `attachments` array; unit test proves it.
- All existing tests still pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md â†’ "PLANNED SCHEMA CHANGES"; `db/001_phase0_migration.sql`; `db/README.md`.
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py` (existing `log_status_change` is the closest
  analog â€” the new `request_history` table supersedes the ad-hoc status log).

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-01.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#1 5â€“6 digit lead IDs (RND-169)
tasks.description:
**task-02 â€” ITEM#1: Accept 5â€“6 digit lead IDs (RND-169)**

**Objective**

The Mercury `@help` bot currently requires exactly a **6-digit** lead ID. Change
it to accept **5 OR 6 digit** lead IDs and reject other lengths / non-numeric,
per SPEC.md Q1.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/handlers.py`:
  - Update `_extract_lead_id(text)` so it matches a 5 **or** 6 digit lead ID
    (still stripping phone patterns first so phone digits are not mistaken for
    a lead ID). Return the matched id as a string, or `None`.
  - Update the `@help` help text and the "missing lead id" prompt to say
    **5â€“6 digit lead ID** (currently says "6-digit").
- Tests: `sources/bot/tests/test_lead_id.py`.

**Acceptance criteria**

- `5` and `6` digit numeric IDs are ACCEPTED (extracted correctly).
- `4` digit and `7`+ digit numbers are REJECTED (not treated as a lead id).
- Non-numeric input is rejected.
- Phone numbers embedded in text (e.g. `555-123-4567`, `(305) 555-1234`,
  bare 10-digit) are still stripped and NOT mistaken for a lead id.
- Help text + missing-id prompt updated to "5â€“6 digit".
- Existing tests still pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q1 (#1 5â€“6 digit IDs, RND-169).
- `sources/bot/handlers.py` â†’ `_extract_lead_id` and `handle_help` (help text +
  the `missing_lead_id` branch near "CREATE NEW REQUEST").

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-02.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#2 dedupe by id_lead + reopen (RND-170)
tasks.description:
**task-03 â€” ITEM#2: Dedupe by id_lead + reopen (RND-170)**

**Objective**

Prevent duplicate requests for the same lead. Per SPEC.md Q2/Q3: store the lead
id on create (already wired), and when a NEW `@help` submission carries an
`id_lead` that already exists on a request, **append a timestamped note to that
existing request instead of creating a new one**. If the matched request is
`Resolved` or `Completed`, **reopen** it (set status â†’ `In Progress`). Write a
`request_history` row for the dedupe/reopen.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`:
  - Add `find_open_or_recent_request_by_lead(id_lead)` (or similar) that returns
    the most relevant existing request for an `id_lead` (define + document the
    match rule; prefer the most recently updated request for that lead).
  - Add `append_note_and_maybe_reopen(request, note_text, actor, actor_name)`
    that appends a timestamped note (reuse the existing note-append format),
    and if the request status is `Resolved`/`Completed`, sets it to
    `In Progress`. Write a `request_history` row (`action='reopen'` when it
    reopens, `action='note'` otherwise) via the task-01 `add_history` shim.
- `sources/bot/handlers.py`:
  - In the CREATE NEW REQU â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- New submission with a lead id that has NO existing request â†’ creates a new
  request (unchanged behaviour).
- New submission with a lead id that HAS an existing open request â†’ appends a
  timestamped note, does NOT create a new request, writes a `note`
  `request_history` row.
- If the matched request was `Resolved`/`Completed` â†’ status becomes
  `In Progress` and a `reopen` `request_history` row is written.
- The bot reply clearly states it updated/reopened request #N.
- Supabase + Slack mocked; no network. Existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q2, Q3 (#2 dedupe + reopen, RND-170).
- `sources/bot/handlers.py` CREATE NEW REQUEST branch + `create_help_request`.
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py` note-append format in `add_request_note` /
  `update_request_status`.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-03.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#3 files/images â†’ Supabase Storage (RND-171)
tasks.description:
**task-04 â€” ITEM#3: Files/images â†’ Supabase Storage (RND-171)**

**Objective**

Let the Mercury `@help` bot capture Slack file/image attachments and store them
durably in the private Supabase Storage bucket `mercury-attachments`, recording
metadata into `requests.attachments`. Per SPEC.md Q4/Q5.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/slack_client.py`:
  - Add `download_slack_file(file_obj_or_url, token)` that fetches a Slack file's
    bytes via the Slack API (uses the file's `url_private_download` with the bot
    token). Return `(bytes, filename, mime, size)`.
  - Add `upload_attachment_to_storage(request_number, filename, data, mime)` that
    uploads to the `mercury-attachments` bucket at a path like
    `requests/<request_number>/<uuid>-<filename>` and returns the stored object
    path (NOT a public URL â€” the bucket is private). Use the Supabase client;
    keep the actual client call thin so it is mockable.
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`:
  - Add `add_attachment(request_number, {filename,url,mi â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- `file #N <with file>` attaches the file to request N and records a well-formed
  attachment object in `requests.attachments`; writes an `attach` history row.
- `file` with no valid `#N` replies asking for the request id and saves nothing.
- File-only post in a request thread attaches to that request.
- File-only NEW post with no id â†’ bot asks for the lead id (saves nothing).
- Slack download + Supabase Storage are mocked; NO network, NO real bucket writes.
- Existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q4, Q5 (#3 files/images, RND-171); `db/README.md` (bucket + signed URLs).
- `sources/bot/slack_client.py` (existing Slack API call pattern);
  `sources/bot/handlers.py` (`note #N` command as a shape reference).

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-04.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#4 standalone notes (RND-172)
tasks.description:
**task-05 â€” ITEM#4: Standalone notes (RND-172)**

**Objective**

Decouple adding a note from changing status, and let the ORIGINAL SUBMITTER (not
just retention) add a note to their own request. Per SPEC.md Q6.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/handlers.py`:
  - The existing `note #N` command is gated behind `is_retention_team`. Loosen
    it so a submitter can add a note to a request THEY submitted (retention/
    supervisor/admin can note any request). Keep the note append working with no
    status change.
  - Ensure the note path writes a `request_history` row (`action='note'`) via
    the shim (coordinate with task-06; if history-writing lands there, at least
    call `add_request_note` which task-06 will instrument).
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`:
  - Confirm `add_request_note` does NOT alter status (it already should) and
    that it is callable for a submitter-owned request. Add an ownership-aware
    h â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- A submitter can `note #N` on a request they submitted; status is unchanged.
- A non-submitter, non-retention user CANNOT note someone else's request.
- Retention/supervisor/admin can note any request.
- Adding a note never changes the request status.
- Supabase/Slack mocked; no network. Existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q6 (#4 standalone notes, RND-172).
- `sources/bot/handlers.py` `note_match` branch; `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`
  `add_request_note`, `is_retention_team`.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-05.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#5 history writes + @help history #N (RND-173)
tasks.description:
**task-06 â€” ITEM#5: Change history + `@help history #N` (RND-173)**

**Objective**

Track ALL changes to a request in `request_history` and expose them via a new
`@help history #N` bot command. Per SPEC.md Q7.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`:
  - Instrument every mutating path to write a `request_history` row via the
    task-01 `add_history` shim: `claim_request`, `assign_request`,
    `update_request_status`, `add_request_note`, `complete_request`, request
    `type` changes, and reopen (from task-03). Each row records
    `actor`, `actor_name`, `action`, `field`, `old_value`, `new_value`.
  - Add `get_request_history(request_number)` reading the NEW `request_history`
    table (ordered by `created_at`) and a `format_history(rows)` helper for
    Slack output. (The old `request_status_history`-based function may be left
    or superseded â€” prefer the new table.)
- `sources/bot/handlers.py`: â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Claim/assign/status/note/type/reopen each write a `request_history` row with
  the correct `action` and old/new values (proven with mocked Supabase).
- `@help history #N` returns a readable, time-ordered history.
- Access: submitter of #N or retention/supervisor/admin can view; others cannot.
- Supabase/Slack mocked; no network. Existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q7 (#5 history, RND-173); task-01 `add_history` shim.
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py` mutating functions + existing
  `log_status_change`/`get_request_history`; `sources/bot/handlers.py` command
  parsing.

---

tasks.name: ITEM#6-display request_type in bot output (RND-174)
tasks.description:
**task-07 â€” ITEM#6 (display): Bot displays request_type (RND-174)**

**Objective**

The request `type` is set ONLY on the dashboard (a dropdown). The bot must be
able to DISPLAY the type in its status/detail output, but never set it. Per
SPEC.md Q8/Q9 (type is optional and never blocks submit/resolve).

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py`:
  - In `format_request_detail` (and `format_request_summary` where sensible),
    show the `request_type` when present. When absent/NULL, omit it gracefully
    (do not print "None").
- `sources/bot/handlers.py`:
  - No new command; ensure the `@help status #N` detail path surfaces the type
    via the formatter. Confirm the bot NEVER writes `request_type`.
- Tests: `sources/bot/tests/test_type_display.py`.

**Acceptance criteria**

- A request WITH a `request_type` shows it in the detail output.
- A request WITHOUT a type shows no type line (no "None"/empty label).
- No bot code path sets/mutates `request_type`.
- Supabase/Slack mocked; no network. Existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q8, Q9 (#6 type, RND-174); the 9 dropdown options list in SPEC.md.
- `sources/bot/supabase_service.py` `format_request_detail` /
  `format_request_summary`.

---

tasks.name: ITEM#8 Performance API (RND-176)
tasks.description:
**task-08 â€” ITEM#8: Performance API (RND-176)**

**Objective**

Add a Retention Command Center backend endpoint that powers the Performance tab:
per-user request status counts plus resolved-today and resolved-this-week, an
"Unassigned" aggregate for unclaimed backlog, and role-scoped visibility. Per
SPEC.md Q11/Q12.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js`:
  - Add `GET /api/retention/performance`. Returns, per user (keyed by
    `assigned_to`, enriched with `mercury_users.display_name`):
    - status counts (Untouched / In Progress / Pending / Resolved / Completed),
    - `resolved_today` and `resolved_this_week` (use the existing eastern-day
      helpers `easternDateStr` / `easternDayRangeUtc` for correct TZ bucketing).
  - Include a synthetic **"Unassigned"** row aggregating requests with no
    `assigned_to` (unclaimed backlog).
  - Role scoping: a `retention` user gets ONLY their own card; `supervisor`/
    `admin` get all cards + the Unassigned card. Derive the requesting user's
    role from `m â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Endpoint returns per-user status counts + resolved-today + resolved-this-week.
- An "Unassigned" aggregate card is present for supervisors/admins.
- A `retention` user's response contains only their own card.
- Week/day counts use the eastern-TZ helpers (no naive UTC bucketing).
- Supabase mocked; no network. Lint + existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q11, Q12 (#8 Performance, RND-176).
- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js` â€” existing `/stats` endpoint and the
  `easternDateStr` / `easternDayRangeUtc` helpers.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-08.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#9 roles + access-filtering API (RND-177)
tasks.description:
**task-09 â€” ITEM#9: Roles + access-filtering API (RND-177)**

**Objective**

Implement the `retention` / `supervisor` / `admin` role model on the Retention
Command Center backend, with server-side filtering so a regular `retention`
user only sees requests assigned to OR created by them, while supervisors/admins
see all. Drive access from `mercury_users` (single source of truth), and
restrict assign/reassign to supervisor/admin. Per SPEC.md Q14/Q15/Q17/Q18.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js`:
  - Add a role helper `getRole(email)` reading `mercury_users` (role âˆˆ
    retention/supervisor/admin; treat admin as supervisor+). Cache briefly if
    convenient.
  - `GET /api/retention/requests` and `/requests/:id`: apply server-side
    scoping. A `retention` user only receives requests where
    `assigned_to == theirSlackId` OR `submitted_by == theirSlackId` (resolve
    their slack id via `mercury_users` by email). Supervisor/admin: no filter.
  - `POST /requests/:id/assign` (and any reassign path): reject with 403 unless
    the caller is supervisor/admin. `claim` may remain open to any retention
    user (claiming self).
  - `GET /api/reten â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- `retention` user: `/requests` returns only their assigned-or-created requests.
- supervisor/admin: `/requests` returns all requests.
- `assign` returns 403 for a `retention` caller; succeeds for supervisor/admin.
- `/access` returns the correct role + tab set, sourced from `mercury_users`.
- A user NOT in `mercury_users` is denied (no access).
- Supabase mocked; no network. Lint + existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q14, Q15, Q17, Q18 (#9 access/roles, RND-177).
- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js` â€” existing `/requests`, `/assign`,
  `/retention-team`, and the `req.user.email` auth context.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-09.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#11 detail lead-join + history API (RND-179)
tasks.description:
**task-10 â€” ITEM#11: Detail lead-join + history API (RND-179)**

**Objective**

Add a Retention Command Center endpoint that, given a request, joins its
`id_lead` to the live lead/case data and returns the FULL `@info` field set,
plus the request's `request_history`. A lead id with no matching record returns
a clean "not found" marker (not an error). Office is derived from the lead/case
lookup, NOT the employees table. Per SPEC.md Q19/Q20/Q21/Q22.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js`:
  - Add `GET /api/retention/requests/:id/detail`. Steps:
    1. Load the request; get its `id_lead`.
    2. Look up the case first (`rep_logs` by `idlead`), then the lead
       (`leads` by `idLead`) â€” mirror the bot's `@info id:` resolution order.
    3. Return the FULL `@info` field set for the matched record. The FIELD
       CONTRACT is the set produced by `sources/bot/detail_formatters.py`
       (`format_case_detail_enhanced` / `format_lead_detail_enhanced`):
       quick view (name, DOA, color tag, converted value, attorney, rep, tx
       location, legal/clinical status, visits), rep info (rep, rep phone,
       supervisor, supervisor phone) â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Endpoint returns the full `@info` field set (structured) for a matching lead/case.
- Field set matches the `detail_formatters.py` contract (no invented fields).
- `request_history` is included.
- No-match lead id â†’ `lead_match:false` + clean message, HTTP 200 (no 500).
- Office is taken from the lead/case record, not employees.
- Supabase mocked; no network. Lint + existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q19, Q20, Q21, Q22 (#11 detail lead+history, RND-179).
- `sources/bot/detail_formatters.py` (THE field contract to reuse).
- `sources/bot/handlers.py` `@info id:` resolution order (case â†’ lead).
- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js` `/requests/:id`.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-10.md)

tasks.name: ITEM#12 filters API (RND-180)
tasks.description:
**task-11 â€” ITEM#12: Filters API (RND-180)**

**Objective**

Extend the Retention Command Center `/requests` endpoint to accept a rich set of
filter params, including filters derived from the joined lead/case record, plus
free-text lead search. Multiple filters combine as **AND**. Per SPEC.md
Q22/Q23/Q24.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js`:
  - `GET /api/retention/requests` accepts (all optional) query params:
    - Request-level: `submitter`, `assigned_to`, `status`, `request_type`,
      `created_after`, `created_before` (created date range).
    - Lead-derived (via the `id_lead` â†’ lead/case join from task-10 logic):
      `office`, `attorney`, `legal_status`, `tx_location` (treatment location).
    - `q` â€” free-text search across lead fields (name/phone/attorney/etc.).
  - Combine ALL provided filters with AND semantics. Request-level filters
    should be pushed to the DB query where possible; lead-derived filters may be
    applied after joining each request's lead/case (keep it c â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Each filter param narrows results correctly in isolation.
- Multiple filters AND-combine (a request must satisfy all provided filters).
- Lead-derived filters (office/attorney/legal_status/tx_location) use the
  lead/case join; office comes from the lead/case, not employees.
- `q` free-text matches across lead fields.
- Role scoping from task-09 is still enforced.
- Supabase mocked; no network. Lint + existing tests pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q22, Q23, Q24 (#12 filters, RND-180).
- `sources/dashboard/routes/retention.js` `/requests`; task-10 lead-join helper.

---
ðŸ“„ [Full prompt fed to the build agent â†’](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/mercury-retention-upgrade/blob/main/build/prompts/task-11.md)

tasks.name: Dashboard frontend A: type dropdown, notes/assign perms, detail panel (RND-174/178/172/179)
tasks.description:
**task-12 â€” Dashboard Frontend A: type dropdown, note/assign perms, detail panel (RND-174 / RND-178 / RND-172 / RND-179)**

**Objective**

Wire the queue page frontend to the new backend: a request-`type` dropdown
(#6), note-by-anyone + assign-supervisor-only controls (#10/#4), and a request
detail panel that shows the joined lead/case info (reusing the `@info` field
layout), the change history, and attachments (#11).

**Files in scope**

- `sources/dashboard/public/retention/queue.html`
- `sources/dashboard/public/retention/js/requests.js`
- `sources/dashboard/public/retention/css/dashboard.css`
- (read) `sources/dashboard/public/retention/js/auth.js`,
  `js/common.js`, `js/supabase-client.js` for the existing patterns.

Implement:
- **Type dropdown (#6):** in the detail/row UI, a dropdown of the 9 request-type
  options (see SPEC.md). Setting it PATCHes the request type via the backend.
  (Add/confirm a backend set-type call; if none exists, use the status/note
  pattern.) Optional â€” blank allowed.
- **Note anyone (#4/#10):** any allowed user can add a note; the note control is
  visible to submitters + retention. Calls the â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Type dropdown lists the 9 options and persists a selection via the backend.
- Note control works for a submitter and for retention.
- Assign control is hidden/disabled for `retention` role; shown for supervisor/admin.
- Detail panel renders the joined lead/case fields (matching the @info sections),
  the history timeline, and attachments; no-match shows the clean message.
- `eslint` passes; any DOM/unit tests you add pass. Bump the `?v=` cache-bust on
  edited client assets referenced in the HTML.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q6/Q8/Q9 (#6), Q6/Q18 (#4/#10), Q19â€“Q22 (#11); the 9 type options list.
- Backend: task-09 `/access`, task-10 `/requests/:id/detail`, existing
  `/requests/:id/note`, `/assign`.
- `sources/bot/detail_formatters.py` (the field layout to mirror);
  `sources/dashboard/public/retention/*`.

tasks.name: Dashboard frontend B: Performance tab + role-gated tabs (RND-176/177)
tasks.description:
**task-13 â€” Dashboard Frontend B: Performance tab + role-gated tabs (RND-176 / RND-177)**

**Objective**

Add the Performance tab (per-user cards + an Unassigned card) and gate the tile's
tabs by role: regular `retention` users see Request / Info / Performance only;
supervisor/admin see all tabs (incl. Staffing/Pulse). The tab allowlist is
driven by `mercury_users` (via the backend `/access` endpoint). Per SPEC.md
Q11/Q12/Q16/Q17.

**Files in scope**

- NEW `sources/dashboard/public/retention/performance.html`
- NEW `sources/dashboard/public/retention/js/performance.js`
- Nav wiring in the existing pages
  (`queue.html`, `staffing.html`, `info.html`) â€” add the Performance tab and
  apply role gating to the nav.
- `sources/dashboard/public/retention/css/dashboard.css` (cards styling).
- (read) `js/auth.js`, `js/common.js`, `js/supabase-client.js`.

Implement:
- **Performance tab (#8):** `performance.js` calls
  `GET /api/retention/performance` and renders one card per user with status
  counts + resolved-today + resolved-this-week, plus a distinct **Unassigned**
  card for unclaimed backlog. A `retention` user sees only their own card
  (b â€¦

**Acceptance criteria**

- Performance page renders per-user cards + an Unassigned card from the API.
- `retention` role: Staffing/Pulse tabs are hidden; only Requests/Info/
  Performance show. supervisor/admin: all tabs show.
- Tab visibility is driven by the backend `/access` role (mercury_users), not a
  static array.
- `?v=` bumped on edited client assets.
- `eslint` passes; any tests you add pass.

**Reference**

- SPEC.md Q11, Q12, Q16, Q17 (#8 Performance, #9 tab gating, RND-176/177).
- Backend: task-08 `/performance`, task-09 `/access`.
- `sources/dashboard/public/retention/queue.html` nav structure;
  `js/supabase-client.js` (`__MERCURY_ALLOWLIST`).

tasks.name: Refresh @help help listing (role-aware capstone) (RND-183)
tasks.description:
**task-14 â€” Refresh the `@help help` listing (role-aware capstone) (RND-183)**

**Objective**

Rewrite the hardcoded `@help help` listing in `handle_help` so it reflects the
FULL, final command set delivered by this build, gated by the new
retention / supervisor / admin role model. This is a documentation-in-lockstep
capstone: several earlier tasks add or widen user-facing `@help` commands but do
NOT touch the help text, so without this task those commands ship functional but
invisible in `@help help`.

Commands that must now appear (added/changed by earlier tasks):
- `@help history #N`      â€” view change history (task-06). Submitter of #N OR
                            retention/supervisor/admin.
- `@help file #N`         â€” attach the posted file(s) to request N (task-04).
- `@help note #N [text]`  â€” now available to the ORIGINAL SUBMITTER on their own
                            request, not just retention (task-05).
- request_type awareness  â€” the dashboard-only type dropdown e â€¦

**Requirements**

1. **Base (all users)** section keeps: submit (5â€“6 digit lead ID), status,
   status #N, my requests, complete #N. ADD the submitter-accessible
   `note #N [text]` here (it is no longer retention-only). ADD `history #N`
   (submitter can view their own request's history). ADD `file #N` (attach a
   posted file to a request the user can act on).
2. **Role-aware extra section.** The current code appends a "Retention Team
   Commands" block when `USE_SUPABASE and is_retention_team(user_id)`. Preserve
   that pattern but make it reflect the NEW role model:
   - Resolve the caller's role via the task-09 role helper / `mercury_users`
     (retention / supervisor / admin). If a direct helper is not importable in
     the bot, fall back to `is_retention_team(user_id)` for the team block and
     `is_assignment_admin(user_id)` (widened to supervisor+admin) for the
     assign line.
   - Team block (retention/supervisor/admin) lists: queue, assigned, claim #N,
     update #N <status>, resolve #N [note], note #N, history #N.
   - Assign/reassign line (`assign #N @user`) shown ONLY to supervisor/admin
     (mirror the existing `is_assignment_admin` gate used by the assign command
     so the help text matches what the user can actually run).
3. Keep the existing Slack mrkdwn formatting style (bold `*...*`, bullet `â€¢`,
   the `_Tip: ..._` footer). Keep messages concise.
4. Do not invent commands that do not exist in the dispatch. Every line in the
   help text MUST correspond to a real handled command in `handle_help`.

**Files in scope**

- `sources/bot/handlers.py` â†’ `handle_help`, ONLY the `help_text` string block
  (the branch guarded by `request_text in ('help','test','?','commands',...)`).
  Do NOT change any command dispatch/parse logic â€” this task is text only.

**Acceptance criteria**

- `@help help` (and aliases `commands`, `?`, etc.) for a NON-retention submitter
  lists: submit, status, status #N, my requests, note #N (own request),
  history #N (own request), complete #N, file #N â€” and does NOT show queue/
  assign/claim.
- For a retention (non-admin) user: the team block additionally shows queue,
  assigned, claim #N, update #N, resolve #N, history #N â€” but NOT
  `assign #N @user`.
- For a supervisor/admin: the team block additionally shows `assign #N @user`.
- Every command listed is one that `handle_help` actually parses and handles.
- No dispatch/parse logic changed; only the `help_text` string(s).
- Existing tests pass; add/extend a test (e.g. `tests/test_help_listing.py`)
  asserting: submitter listing contains "history" and "file" and "note"; the
  assign line only appears under the admin/supervisor gate (mock the role
  helpers). Mock Slack/Supabase; no network.

**Reference**

- `sources/bot/handlers.py` `handle_help` (help_text block ~lines 274â€“312);
  role gates `is_retention_team`, `is_assignment_admin`.
- task-04 (`file #N`), task-05 (submitter `note #N`), task-06 (`history #N`),
  task-09 (retention/supervisor/admin role model + widened assign gate).
- SPEC.md Q14/Q18 (roles + who may assign).
